fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Tennessee Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Tennessee - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity tennessee, how to transfer occupational therapist license to tennessee, occupational therapist compact tennessee, tennessee occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Tennessee Board of Occupational Therapy
board.mailing_address:
Tennessee Department of Health
Health Related Boards
665 Mainstream Drive
Nashville, TN 37423

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Tennessee.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create Account & Apply Online
steps.description: Register for an account and complete the application via the Tennessee Department of Health Licensure System. Pay the required application fee of $100.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts, verifying your degree and date conferred, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Tennessee Board of Occupational Therapy.

steps.title: Verify NBCOT Certification
steps.description: Request verification of your certification from the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) to be sent directly to the Tennessee Board of Occupational Therapy.

steps.title: Complete Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided within the Tennessee Department of Health Licensure System to complete the required criminal background check.

steps.title: Pass Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Take and pass the Tennessee Jurisprudence Exam online as part of your application process.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Tennessee OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Tennessee have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Tennessee has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Tennessee?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Tennessee while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in Tennessee
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Tennessee, submit your application online through the Tennessee Department of Health Licensure System.

official_links.portal_name: Tennessee Department of Health Licensure System
official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the portal URL and click on 'Register a New Account' at the bottom of the page to create your user account before starting the application.
